text: In a quiet Scottish town stands an old oak tree. Every autumn leaves turn its branches orange. The squirrels play chase around its trunk. One autumn day a gust big enough blows. Its branches snap. It comes crashing down. Hawks circle nearby. They think beef burgers. The squirrels think beans. They run home. Later stars twinkle. Owls hoot. Cats sleep. Birds sing. Summer passes. Tree sprouts again. Leaves grow green.
